Title: Create return from existing order
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. At least one order exists with shipped items
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Click 'Add new return' button
3. Select an existing order from the order picker
4. Select one or more items to return
5. Enter return reason and quantity
6. Click Save
Expected Result:
1. Return is created successfully with status 'New'
2. Return number is auto-generated
3. Return appears in the returns grid
4. Linked order number is displayed correctly
5. Item count matches selected items

Title: View return details
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. At least one return exists in the grid
Steps: 1. Navigate to Returns workspace\n2. Click on an existing return row in the grid
Expected Result:
1. Return detail blade opens
2. All fields are displayed: Return number, Order number, Customer, Status, Create date, Modify date, Created by, Item count
3. Return items list is visible with quantities and reasons
4. Data matches what is shown in the grid row

Title: Edit return details
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. A return in 'New' status exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Open an existing return in 'New' status
3. Modify the return reason or item quantity
4. Click Save
5. Close the blade and reopen the same return
Expected Result:
1. Save completes without errors
2. Modify date is updated
3. Changes are persisted after closing and reopening the blade
4. API PUT request returns 200/204 status code

Title: Delete a return
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. A return in 'New' status exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Select a return in 'New' status
3. Click Delete button
4. Confirm the deletion dialog
Expected Result:
1. Return is removed from the grid
2. Grid refreshes and return no longer appears
3. Attempting to access the deleted return by direct URL shows appropriate error
4. API DELETE request returns 200/204

Title: Search returns by return number and order number
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. Multiple returns exist in the grid
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Enter a known return number in the search field
3. Verify filtered results
4. Clear search
5. Enter a known order number in the search field
6. Verify filtered results
Expected Result:
1. Search by return number shows only matching returns
2. Search by order number shows returns linked to that order
3. Clearing search restores full list
4. Search is case-insensitive
5. No results message displayed when search has no matches

Title: Transition return status from New to Processing
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. A return exists in 'New' status
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Open a return in 'New' status
3. Change status to 'Processing'
4. Click Save
5. Verify the status in the grid
Expected Result:
1. Status changes to 'Processing' successfully
2. Modify date is updated
3. Grid reflects the new status
4. Status change is persisted after page refresh

Title: Transition return status from Processing to Completed
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. A return exists in 'Processing' status
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Open a return in 'Processing' status
3. Change status to 'Completed'
4. Click Save
5. Verify the status in the grid
Expected Result:
1. Status changes to 'Completed' successfully
2. Modify date is updated
3. Grid reflects the new status
4. Completed return becomes read-only or restricts further edits

Title: Transition return status from Processing to Rejected
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. A return exists in 'Processing' status
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Open a return in 'Processing' status
3. Change status to 'Rejected'
4. Click Save
5. Verify the status in the grid
Expected Result:
1. Status changes to 'Rejected' successfully
2. Modify date is updated
3. Grid reflects the new status
4. Rejected return becomes read-only or restricts further edits

Title: Illegal status transition from Completed to New is blocked
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. A return exists in 'Completed' status
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Open a return in 'Completed' status
3. Attempt to change status back to 'New' or 'Processing'
4. Attempt to save if status dropdown allows the change
Expected Result:
1. Status dropdown does not offer 'New' or 'Processing' as options for a Completed return
2. If dropdown allows selection, save should fail with a validation error
3. No backward transitions are permitted in the state machine
4. Same restriction applies for Rejected returns

Title: Filter returns grid by status
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. Returns exist in multiple statuses (New, Processing, Completed, Rejected)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Apply filter for 'New' status
3. Verify only New returns are shown
4. Change filter to 'Processing'
5. Change filter to 'Completed'
6. Remove all filters
Expected Result:
1. Each status filter shows only returns matching that status
2. Return count updates to match filtered results
3. Removing filters restores the full list
4. Filters can be combined if multi-select is supported

Title: Create return without selecting an order fails validation
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Click 'Add new return'
3. Do not select any order
4. Attempt to proceed or save
Expected Result:
1. Validation error is displayed indicating an order is required
2. Return is not created
3. Save button is disabled or form submission is blocked
4. Error message is clear and user-friendly

Title: Duplicate return for same order items is prevented
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. A return already exists for specific items of an order
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Click 'Add new return'
3. Select the same order that already has a return
4. Select the same items that are already in an existing return
5. Attempt to save
Expected Result: 1. System either prevents selecting already-returned items or shows a warning\n2. If duplicate is allowed, a clear warning message is displayed\n3. Total returned quantity across all returns should not exceed ordered quantity

Title: Return quantity exceeding order quantity is rejected
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. An order exists with item quantity of N
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Click 'Add new return'
3. Select an order
4. Select an item and enter return quantity greater than ordered quantity
5. Attempt to save
Expected Result:
1. Validation error is displayed: return quantity cannot exceed ordered quantity
2. Return is not created
3. Input field highlights the error
4. Maximum allowed quantity is indicated to the user

Title: Required fields validation on return creation
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Click 'Add new return'
3. Select an order but leave return reason empty
4. Leave item quantity at zero or empty
5. Attempt to save
Expected Result:
1. Validation errors are displayed for all empty required fields
2. Return is not created until all required fields are filled
3. Each required field has a clear error message
4. Save button is disabled or form blocks submission

Title: Return is linked to the correct order
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. A return exists linked to a known order
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Open a return and note the Order number
3. Click on the Order number link or navigate to Orders workspace
4. Locate the same order
5. Verify the order details match
Expected Result:
1. Order number in return matches actual order
2. Clicking order number navigates to the correct order blade
3. Order items match the items referenced in the return
4. Customer information is consistent between return and order

Title: Customer name on return matches linked order customer
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. Multiple returns exist for different customers
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Note the Customer column for several returns
3. Open each return and verify customer name
4. Cross-reference with the linked order's customer
Expected Result:
1. Customer name in the grid matches the customer in the return detail
2. Customer name matches the customer on the linked order
3. No mismatched or empty customer fields
4. Customer data is consistent across Returns and Orders modules

Title: Completed return triggers inventory restoration
Preconditions:
1. Admin user is logged in
2. Track inventory is enabled
3. An order exists with items that decremented stock
4. A return in 'Processing' status exists for that order
Steps:
1. Note current inventory count for the returned item
2. Navigate to Returns workspace
3. Open the return in 'Processing' status
4. Change status to 'Completed'
5. Save the return
6. Navigate to Inventory module
7. Check the stock quantity for the returned item
Expected Result:
1. Inventory quantity increases by the returned item quantity
2. Inventory adjustment is logged if change logging is enabled
3. Stock count matches: previous count + returned quantity
4. Fulfillment center stock is updated correctly

Title: Refund amount calculation matches returned items
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. A return exists with items that have known prices
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Open a return with multiple items
3. Verify the refund amount displayed
4. Manually calculate: sum of (item price x return quantity) for each item
5. Compare calculated amount with displayed refund amount
Expected Result:
1. Refund amount equals the sum of returned item prices multiplied by quantities
2. Currency formatting is correct
3. If applicable, taxes and discounts are accounted for in the refund
4. Refund amount is consistent between return detail and any linked payment refund

Title: Sort returns grid by all sortable columns
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. Multiple returns exist with different values
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Click on 'Return number' column header to sort ascending
3. Click again to sort descending
4. Repeat for: Order number, Customer, Return status, Create date, Modify date, Created by, Item count
Expected Result:
1. Each column sorts correctly in ascending and descending order
2. Sort indicator (arrow) shows current sort direction
3. Numeric columns sort numerically, dates sort chronologically, text sorts alphabetically
4. Grid updates without errors after each sort

Title: Pagination works correctly in returns grid
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. More returns exist than the default page size (e.g., more than 20)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Verify the default page shows limited results
3. Click next page or scroll to load more
4. Navigate back to the first page
5. Change page size if option is available
Expected Result:
1. Pagination controls are displayed when results exceed page size
2. Navigating pages shows correct data without duplicates
3. Total count is displayed and accurate
4. Page size change refreshes the grid with the correct number of rows

Title: Grid column visibility and data completeness
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. Returns exist in the grid
Steps: 1. Navigate to Returns workspace\n2. Verify all expected columns are visible: Return number, Order number, Customer, Return status, Create date, Modify date, Created by, Item count\n3. Check that no column has missing or null data for existing returns
Expected Result:
1. All 8 columns are visible in the grid
2. No columns show empty or 'undefined' values for existing returns
3. Column widths allow data to be readable without excessive truncation
4. Date columns display in a consistent format

Title: Bulk select and delete multiple returns
Preconditions: 1. Admin user is logged in\n2. Multiple returns in 'New' status exist
Steps:
1. Navigate to Returns workspace
2. Select multiple returns using checkboxes
3. Click the Delete button
4. Confirm the bulk deletion dialog
5. Verify the grid after deletion
Expected Result:
1. Multiple returns can be selected via checkboxes
2. Delete button is enabled when returns are selected
3. Confirmation dialog shows the count of returns to be deleted
4. All selected returns are removed from the grid after confirmation
5. Non-selected returns remain unaffected
